mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the CPI above 2.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and increases inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with both declining federal funds rate and rising CPI are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial jobless claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial jobless claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening supply chain and labor market that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with both declining industrial production and rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening economy that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
